Yale Applied Cryptography Laboratory

We are a group of researchers at Yale working on applied cryptography.

Our Team Weekly Seminar

Updates

Dec 29, 2025 paper InstantOMR: Oblivious Message Retrieval with Low Latency and Optimal Parallelizability accepted to USENIX Security '26; Cirrus: Performant and Accountable Distributed SNARK accepted to NDSS '26.
Dec 29, 2025 news Arthur Lazzaretti successfully defended his PhD and joined xAI.
Nov 8, 2025 paper Aviv Yaish's paper "Inequality in the Age of Pseudonymity" accepted to AAAI '26.
Nov 8, 2025 award Aviv Yaish received three grants from the Ethereum Foundation and Columbia Business School Digital Future Initiative, and was named to CBER Forum's Rising Stars and Hebrew University's 40 Under 40.
Sep 1, 2025 news Kaihua Qin accepted as Assistant Professor at the University of Warwick.
Aug 30, 2025 paper Multiple papers accepted at TCC '25, Asiacrypt '25, CCS '25, Eurocrypt '25, and CSF '25 on private information retrieval, lattice-based cryptography, and blockchain economics.
May 20, 2025 news Georgios Tsimos successfully defended his PhD and joined Pod Network as a scientist.
Apr 4, 2025 talk 2nd PAVE Blockchain Symposium announced for April 11th.
Mar 17, 2025 news Multiple YACL members joined IC3 (Intercontinental Crypto Collaborative).
Mar 15, 2025 paper Three papers accepted to IEEE S&P '25 covering Ethereum's builder market, data availability, and vector commitments.
Feb 10, 2025 paper Two papers accepted to USENIX Security '25 on Byzantine Fault Tolerance and lattice-based leader election.
Feb 7, 2025 paper Three papers accepted to Eurocrypt '25 on SNARKs, secret sharing, and public-key encryption.
Jan 31, 2025 talk Spring seminar series launched.
Jan 13, 2025 news Jiasun Li joining as visiting professor for the spring semester.
Dec 6, 2024 paper Multiple papers accepted to FC '25 on zero-knowledge proofs, auctions, and blockchain protocols.
